title: rhagadiolus hamosus
intro: Rhagadiolus hamosus, also known as the marsh marigold of the sea or sea holly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Rhagadiolus hamosus has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Rhagadiolus hamosus can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
